title: How to use this API key
subtitle: Copy-paste configuration for popular clients. Requests are routed by model name through this gateway.
keyNotice: Use the key you copied when you created this token. For security the full key is shown only once at creation and cannot be retrieved again — the examples below use the placeholder `sk-YOUR_API_KEY`, so replace it with your real key.
modelNote: The model below is just an example. Use a model that one of your channels actually serves — list available models with `GET {{apiBase}}/models`.
routingNote: This key belongs to group **{{group}}**, so it routes to channels configured for that group.

notFound.title: Key not found
notFound.desc: This API key no longer exists or you do not have access to it.

copy.failed: Could not copy automatically — select and copy manually.

claudeCode.body:
Claude Code speaks the Anthropic Messages API. Point it at this gateway and use your key as the auth token, then launch `claude`:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL={{baseUrl}}
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=sk-YOUR_API_KEY
# optional: pin the model Claude Code should use
export ANTHROPIC_MODEL={{model}}
claude
```

The gateway accepts the credential Claude Code sends (both `x-api-key` and `Authorization: Bearer` work). Requests hit `{{apiBase}}/messages` and are routed by model name.

codex.body:
Codex CLI is OpenAI-compatible. The quickest way is environment variables:

```bash
export OPENAI_BASE_URL={{apiBase}}
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-YOUR_API_KEY
codex --model {{model}}
```

Or define a custom provider in `~/.codex/config.toml`:

```toml
model = "{{model}}"
model_provider = "agent-router"

[model_providers.agent-router]
name = "Agent Router"
base_url = "{{apiBase}}"
env_key = "OPENAI_API_KEY"
```

Config keys vary slightly between Codex versions — check `codex --help` if a field name differs.

litellm.body:
Use the `openai/` prefix so litellm calls this gateway over the OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

```python
import litellm

resp = litellm.completion(
    model="openai/{{model}}",
    api_base="{{apiBase}}",
    api_key="sk-YOUR_API_KEY",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
)
print(resp.choices[0].message.content)
```

openai.body:
Point the official OpenAI Python SDK at the gateway base URL:

```python
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(base_url="{{apiBase}}", api_key="sk-YOUR_API_KEY")
resp = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="{{model}}",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
)
print(resp.choices[0].message.content)
```

Streaming works too — pass `stream=True` and iterate the chunks.

curl.body:
The gateway exposes both an OpenAI-compatible endpoint and the Anthropic Messages endpoint.

**OpenAI-compatible** (`Authorization: Bearer`):

```bash
curl {{apiBase}}/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "{{model}}",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
  }'
```

**Anthropic Messages** (`x-api-key` + `anthropic-version`):

```bash
curl {{apiBase}}/messages \
  -H "x-api-key: sk-YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "{{model}}",
    "max_tokens": 1024,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
  }'
```
